title: Adding images to automated emails
excerpt: While creating an automation to send an email when a new item is created on a table, an image should be able to added to the email. For now, you can only add an image as a attachment document, however it’s better to add …

posts.author: senahoroz (Sena Horoz)
posts.cookedHtml: While creating an automation to send an email when a new item is created on a table, an image should be able to added to the email. For now, you can only add an image as a attachment document, however it’s better to add in into the mail body. Also, the attached document can only be opend by Monday users, for now. But, It should be open to everbody. I am tying to create an automated welcome email to my clients, but I cannot add an welcome-image to my email.

posts.author: KenVB (Ken VanBree)
posts.cookedHtml:
Sena,
I agree with you that it is better to embed the image in the email. Have you found a way to do it?
Ken VanBree

posts.author: senahoroz (Sena Horoz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Ken,

Unfortunately we cannot. So, we do not add any image to our emails.
We will integrate mailchimp for the marketing emails, so that we can add as much images and other cool stuff as we want.

Fyi,
Best Regards

Ken VanBree via monday Community Forum <notifications@monday.discoursemail.com>, 22 Haz 2023 Per, 17:29 tarihinde şunu yazdı:

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml: @senahoroz How can you use mailchimp for marketing emails out of monday? I saw integrations but nothing about emailing out of monday

posts.author: theshiftybadger (Chase Baker)
posts.cookedHtml: ^ this. using Make was our solution to the problem, and it actually opened our eyes to a vastly larger world of possibilities when it comes to automations in general. not to mention how much absurdly faster Make scenarios are compared to native Monday automations: in one use case we saw a series of automations for an approval workflow go down from ~3 minutes on average in Monday for the series to run versus 8 seconds in Make.

posts.author: Yash.Gangani (Yash Gangani)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @senahoroz

Check out EasyMails where you can design your email template via drag and drop editor.
You can add images, tables and lots of other things.

posts.author: dharmik (Dharmik Jagodana)
posts.cookedHtml: Check out imejis.io if you’re looking to create dynamic and personalized images for every email you send.

posts.author: shirozayas (Shiro Zayas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Checkout SuperMail how send files as attachments from your monday.com board into outgoing emails.

You can attched constant files or dynamice files directly from your board - check out this short tutorial

posts.author: kevin_vaghasiya (kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @senahoroz @KenVB,

You can use EasyMails, it allows you to add image in email body with drag and drop template builder.
